name: Apple, Prune and Armagnac Cake with Streusel Topping
recipeIngredient: 12 ounces Ready To Use Prunes (without stones)
2 ounces Whole Almonds, halved and shredded
8 inch Tin with a Loose Base, greased and lined with greaseproof paper
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to gas mark 4, 350°F, 180°C.
Soak the ready-to-use prunes in sugar and water for 15 minutes, then remove and add Armagnac. Stir well, cover, and leave overnight.
Make the streusel topping: Sift flour and butter into a bowl and rub until crumb-like. Add sugar evenly, sprinkle cold water, and fork until coarse and lumpy. Set aside with almonds.
Sift flour and baking powder into a bowl. Add remaining ingredients (except apple) and beat smooth using an electric hand whisk or wooden spoon. Fold in apple, spoon mixture into prepared tin.
Arrange prunes over the mixture, fork streusel topping over prunes, sprinkle shredded almonds evenly. Place cake on center shelf of oven, bake for 1 hour, then remove. Let cool in tin for 30 minutes before turning out onto a wire rack.
Sift icing sugar over surface just before serving.
